trans-unit.source: 🚧 This section of the training platform and the entire content is under ⚠️construction⚠️ and may not be shared or published! 🚧
trans-unit.target: 🚧 This section of the training platform and the entire content is under ⚠️construction⚠️ and may not be shared or published! 🚧

trans-unit.source: Task 4: Something something capacity?
trans-unit.target: Task 4: Something something capacity?

trans-unit.source: STEP 1: Data Preparation
trans-unit.target: STEP 1: Data Preparation

trans-unit.source: Download population density layer here: https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=49708 and open in QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Download population density layer here: https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=49708 and open in QGIS.

trans-unit.source: The units are the number of people per pixel. Country totals have been adjusted to match the corresponding official United Nations population estimates produced by the Population Division of the Economic and Social Affairs Department of the United Nations Secretariat (2019 Revision of World Population Prospects). "NoData" values represent areas that have been mapped unpopulated. This was done based on building footprints from the Digitize Africa project by Ecopia.AI and Maxar Technologies (2020).
trans-unit.target: The units are the number of people per pixel. Country totals have been adjusted to match the corresponding official United Nations population estimates produced by the Population Division of the Economic and Social Affairs Department of the United Nations Secretariat (2019 Revision of World Population Prospects). "NoData" values represent areas that have been mapped unpopulated. This was done based on building footprints from the Digitize Africa project by Ecopia.AI and Maxar Technologies (2020).

trans-unit.source: Create a vector grid
trans-unit.target: Create a vector grid

trans-unit.source: make sure to change the coordinate system so that you can enter the data in meters instead of degrees https://mangomap.com/robertyoung/maps/69585/what-utm-zone-am-i-in-# here you are able to calculate the specific UTM zone https://projectionwizard.org/ if you zoom into the respective region you will get the appropriate projection
trans-unit.target: make sure to change the coordinate system so that you can enter the data in meters instead of degrees https://mangomap.com/robertyoung/maps/69585/what-utm-zone-am-i-in-# here you are able to calculate the specific UTM zone https://projectionwizard.org/ if you zoom into the respective region you will get the appropriate projection

trans-unit.source: Create a vector grid with 1000m resolution by using the tool create grid
trans-unit.target: Create a vector grid with 1000m resolution by using the tool create grid

trans-unit.source: Join the grid and the population layer by using the tool zonal statistics
trans-unit.target: Join the grid and the population layer by using the tool zonal statistics

trans-unit.source: Input layer: Grid Networkanalysis - Hospital Accessibility, Rwanda.zip Raster layer: Population layer statistics to calculate: sum
trans-unit.target: Input layer: Grid Networkanalysis - Hospital Accessibility, Rwanda.zip Raster layer: Population layer statistics to calculate: sum

trans-unit.source: Cut the Grid along the border of Rwanda by using the tool select by location to make sure that we just work with the objects with the country border. Administrative boundaries can be downloaded here: https://www.naturalearthdata.com/
trans-unit.target: Cut the Grid along the border of Rwanda by using the tool select by location to make sure that we just work with the objects with the country border. Administrative boundaries can be downloaded here: https://www.naturalearthdata.com/

trans-unit.source: extract features from: zonal statistic layer (Grid) where the features: intersect by comparing to the features from: Rwanda border layer
trans-unit.target: extract features from: zonal statistic layer (Grid) where the features: intersect by comparing to the features from: Rwanda border layer

trans-unit.source: ADDIT: In addition, it makes sense to have a look on the a basemap like OSM to check if there are any big water surfaces etc. Therefore, you can also use the tool QuickOSM and use the name of the water body. If so, you should cut them out, as water areas are uninhabited (population layer). Use the tool select by location.
trans-unit.target: ADDIT: In addition, it makes sense to have a look on the a basemap like OSM to check if there are any big water surfaces etc. Therefore, you can also use the tool QuickOSM and use the name of the water body. If so, you should cut them out, as water areas are uninhabited (population layer). Use the tool select by location.

trans-unit.source: Creation of hospital layer by using the the tool QuickOSM
trans-unit.target: Creation of hospital layer by using the the tool QuickOSM

trans-unit.source: key = amenity … value = hospital … in … Rwanda → Result: point-, line- & polygon layer
trans-unit.target: key = amenity … value = hospital … in … Rwanda → Result: point-, line- & polygon layer

trans-unit.source: using the tool „select by location“ we can check if there are objects from the polygon or line layer that are not included in the point layer
trans-unit.target: using the tool „select by location“ we can check if there are objects from the polygon or line layer that are not included in the point layer

trans-unit.source: if so; we need to convert them into points
trans-unit.target: if so; we need to convert them into points

trans-unit.source: by using the tool centroids, which creates a new point layer with the points representing the centers of the geometries of the input layer
trans-unit.target: by using the tool centroids, which creates a new point layer with the points representing the centers of the geometries of the input layer

trans-unit.source: by using the tool „merge vector layers“ we merge the first point layer and the new point layer
trans-unit.target: by using the tool „merge vector layers“ we merge the first point layer and the new point layer

trans-unit.source: STEP 2: Isochrone generation
trans-unit.target: STEP 2: Isochrone generation

trans-unit.source: by using the tool ORS tools we calculate the isochrones for the hospitals
trans-unit.target: by using the tool ORS tools we calculate the isochrones for the hospitals

trans-unit.source: STEP 3: Merge Population and Isochrones
trans-unit.target: STEP 3: Merge Population and Isochrones

trans-unit.source: ADDIT: Remember to rename the layers to keep the overview. The attribute tables should also be clear. Delete irrelevant columns and rename columns if necessary.
trans-unit.target: ADDIT: Remember to rename the layers to keep the overview. The attribute tables should also be clear. Delete irrelevant columns and rename columns if necessary.

trans-unit.source: STEP 4: Calculate available hospitals in 60 Min.
trans-unit.target: STEP 4: Calculate available hospitals in 60 Min.

trans-unit.source: Open the attribute table of the final and use the field calculator.
trans-unit.target: Open the attribute table of the final and use the field calculator.

trans-unit.source: Step 5 Analysis of potential patient volume (lower level)
trans-unit.target: Step 5 Analysis of potential patient volume (lower level)

trans-unit.source: 1: Create a new ID for the hospitals
trans-unit.target: 1: Create a new ID for the hospitals

trans-unit.source: 2: Export the final grid as Excel File
trans-unit.target: 2: Export the final grid as Excel File

trans-unit.source: Export the layer final grid as MS Office Open XMP Spreadsheet [XLSX)
trans-unit.target: Export the layer final grid as MS Office Open XMP Spreadsheet [XLSX)

trans-unit.source: 3: Open file and insert pivot table
trans-unit.target: 3: Open file and insert pivot table

trans-unit.source: 4: Join
trans-unit.target: 4: Join
